container.title: Part A. Science specified study
Mathematics and statistics unit
STA1010 Statistical methods for science
container.description: STA1010 Statistical methods for science is recommended if you are planning to study areas where experimental design and data analysis skills are particularly important, such as the life sciences. STA1010 requires VCE Mathematical Methods Units 3 and 4 (or equivalent), or SCI1020 or MTH1010.

parent_record.value: Curriculum Structure Container: Mathematics and statistics unit

container.title: SCI1022 Introduction to scientific coding
container.description: SCI1022 Introduction to scientific coding is recommended if you want to gain fundamental coding skills that can be applied in a variety of scientific areas. It will be useful for data analysis, modelling of complex systems, data manipulation and visualisation in a scientific context. SCI1022 requires a raw study score of at least 25 in VCE Mathematical Methods Units 3 and 4 (or equivalent) or STA1010.

parent_record.value: Curriculum Structure Container: Mathematics and statistics unit

container.title: SCI1020 Introduction to statistical reasoning
container.description: SCI1020 Introduction to statistical reasoning is recommended if you are planning to study areas where experimental design and data analysis skills are particularly important, such as the life sciences. SCI1020 is for students without a strong mathematics background.

parent_record.value: Curriculum Structure Container: Mathematics and statistics unit

container.title: MTH1020 Analysis of change
container.description: MTH1020 Analysis of change is recommended/ required if you have an interest in astrophysics, some streams of climate and atmospheric science, computational science, mathematics and/or physics. MTH1020 requires a raw study score of at least 25 in VCE Mathematical Methods (or equivalent).

parent_record.value: Curriculum Structure Container: Mathematics and statistics unit

container.title: MTH1030 Techniques for modelling
container.description:
MTH1030 Techniques for modelling is recommended/required if you have an interest in astrophysics, some streams of climate and atmospheric science, computational science, mathematics and/or physics. MTH1030 requires a raw study score of at least 30 in VCE Specialist Mathematics (or equivalent) or MTH1020.

If you have a strong mathematics background and a keen interest in the subject you could replace the unit MTH1030 with the advanced version MTH1035. You must enrol in this unit manually via the online Enrolment Amendment form.

parent_record.value: Curriculum Structure Container: Mathematics and statistics unit

container.title: Information on double degree courses
container.description:
You are exempt from completing the mathematics and statistics unit in the following double degree courses when you complete a corresponding mathematics unit in the requirements of the partner course as listed below:

B2023 Bachelor of Commerce and Bachelor of Science - ETC1000 Business and economic statistics or ETX1100 Business statistics
E3007 Bachelor of Engineering (Honours) and Bachelor of Science - ENG1005 Engineering mathematics

parent_record.value: Curriculum Structure Container: Mathematics and statistics unit

container.description:
You must complete at least one of the following Level 1 mathematics or statistics units if not already taken as part of a Level 1 approved sequence. If you have already completed 6 credit points of the following units as part of a level 1 approved sequence, you must replace the Mathematics and statistics unit with a level 1 science unit (6 credit points).

- Please ensure that you have met the pre-requisites for your chosen Level 1 mathematics and statistics unit. For further guidance on your mathematics and statistics unit selection you can refer to the guide provided in the Structure section under Course progression map.
- You must complete this requirement within the first 48 credit points of a single Science course and within the first 72 credit points of a Science combined (double degree) course. For exceptions, please see information on double degree courses below.

parent_record.value: Curriculum Structure Container: Level 1 science sequences

container.description:
You must complete at least two approved Level 1 sequences from the disciplines listed below. The choice of Level 1 sequences will impact your choice of Level 2 and Level 3 units and will normally lay the foundation for a related major. The two Level 1 sequences must be selected from separate disciplines and cannot have units in common.

If you are studying a single degree course, it is recommended that you take a third Level 1 sequence in the first year to increase your options for choice of major. If you are completing a double degree course you will not be able to fit in three Level 1 sequences.

parent_record.value: Curriculum Structure Container: Part A. Science specified study

container.description: Although the requirements in Part A appear to exceed 48 points, a level 1 sequence is typically counted towards the chosen major in Part B.

container.title: Rules
container.description:
Note 1: In choosing the 96 credit points of Bachelor of Science listed study over Part A and Part B, you must ensure that you complete six science listed units (36 credit points) at Level 1, and ten science listed units (60 credit points) at Levels 2 and 3 with a minimum of four units (24 credit points) at Level 3.

The six Level 1 science listed units (36 credit points) include those Level 1 units completed as part of your science specified study. You may also chose Level 1 units from any major, extended major or minor within the Bachelor of Science—except for the business units listed under the Food Science and Business extended major in Malaysia, which are classified as science units only for students enrolled in that specific extended major—or from Level 1 mathematics and statistics units or from the additional Level 1 science units provided. Similarly, the ten Level 2 and 3 Bachelor of Science listed units (60 credit points) include the Level 2 and 3 units taken as part of your major in Part B, with at least 4 units (24 credit points) at Level 3. You may also choose Level 2 and 3 units from the list of units under Years two and three, as needed. 

Note 2: You must ensure that across the whole course, you complete no more than ten Level 1 units (60 credit points).

Note 3: To progress in the course you must complete the Level 1 sequences at stage one before enrolling in Level 2 science units.

container.title: Part B. Science listed major
Science extended majors
container.description:
You may complete a science extended major, provided you have sufficient room in your course.

You may also use free space in your course to extend your science major to form an extended major.

The Psychology extended major (APAC accredited Level 1 sequence of units) is only offered in the Bachelor of Science (S2000) single degree or in the Bachelor of Science and Bachelor of Arts (S2006) double degree. The Psychology extended major (APAC accredited Level 1 sequence of units) is required if you intend to progress to honours in Psychology.

Please note, science extended majors are not available within the Bachelor of Science (M2021) double degree.

For other students undertaking a double degree: Some extended majors are not available in certain double degrees. Please check the 'Availability' section of each extended major for information.

container.title: Science minors

You may complete a science listed minor provided you have enough room in your course.

Double degree students note: Some minors are not available in certain double degrees, check the Availability section of each minor.

parent_record.value: Curriculum Structure Container: Part B. Science listed major

container.description:
You must complete at least one of the following majors.

A major requires eight units with no more than two units at Level 1 (12 credit points) and at least three units (18 credit points) at Level 3. You may replace the major with an extended major by using the elective units available in Part A. or Part C.

It is recommended that you consider completing Level 2 units in more than one area of science to maximise your choice of major at Level 3.

If you are intending to undertake an honours year you should ensure that you complete the specific units required as a prerequisite for honours in your chosen major area of study.

For students undertaking a double degree: Some majors are not available in certain double degrees. Please check the 'Availability' section of each major.

container.title: Part C. Free elective study
container.description:
Elective units may be chosen from the remaining units available in the Bachelor of Science course and will enable you to extend your major or to complete a second major or minor(s). 

Elective units may also be taken from non-science disciplines to broaden knowledge or to complete a major or minor(s) from another course as long as you have the prerequisites and there are no restrictions on enrolment in the units.

You may need permission from the owning faculty to enrol in some units taught by other faculties.

Note: No more than two units (12 credit points) can be credited towards two majors, or a major and a minor. The same credit points cannot be credited towards more than one minor.
